reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The 'Contents' page (Page 3 of the source PDF) is entirely missing from the output. Additionally, other pages such as Page 4 (blank page) and Page 17 (blank page) are absent. The omission of the Table of Contents page is a critical loss of source information.
concerns.output_quote: [The output transitions directly from Page 2's blank page text to the 'Legislative Reporting Requirement' section, skipping the Contents page completely.]

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There is a systemic omission of internal subheadings throughout the document, particularly those titled 'Completed Activities', 'Planned Activities', and 'Development Activities'. While the paragraphs following these headings are usually present, the headings themselves (which serve as key navigational and structural anchors in the source) are missing in many sections starting from page 8 through page 15. This constitutes a significant loss of organizational text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Completed Activities (under State Health Plan, page 10); Planned Activities (under State Health Plan, page 10); Planned Activities (under Medicaid, page 11); Completed Activities (under CFS, page 12); Planned Activities (under CFS, page 12); etc.
concerns.output_quote: [Subheadings are omitted, with text from different sub-sections merged sequentially without clear delineators.]
concerns.location: Sections for Health Care, Longitudinal and Performance Systems, and Enterprise Solution Development (Pages 10-15)

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy in the output is inconsistent and breaks the logical structure established in the source's Table of Contents (page 3). In the source, 'CJLEADS' and 'Criminal Investigative Case Management' are sibling sub-sections under 'Criminal Justice and Public Safety'. However, the output assigns 'CJLEADS' to H3 and its sibling 'Criminal Investigative Case Management' to H4. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table of Contents (Page 3) and Body Text (Pages 6-7) show 'CJLEADS' / 'Criminal Investigative Case Management' and 'Child Protective Services Pilot' / 'Juvenile Justice' as sibling sub-sections.
concerns.output_quote: "text": "CJLEADS", "level": 3 vs "text": "Criminal Investigative Case Management", "level": 4. Also "text": "Child Protective Services Pilot", "level": 3 vs "text": "Juvenile Justice", "level": 4.
concerns.location: Sections 'Criminal Justice and Public Safety' and 'Child and Family Safety', elements heading-3, heading-6, heading-8, and heading-9.

concerns.analysis: The output contains several 'artifact' tables in Appendix B that do not exist as tables in the source. Text labels from the Gantt chart (Portfolio) have been misidentified as tables, causing single words or phrases to be split across cells (e.g., 'NC Dept of Re' | 'venue'). This corrupts the structural integrity of the chart's data representation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Appendix B (Page 19) contains a graphical Gantt chart with labels like 'NC Dept of Revenue', 'DHHS Medicaid Pilot', and 'OSBM Transparency'.
concerns.output_quote: Three tables at the end of the JSON splitting these labels, such as: "text": "NC Dept of Re" (cell 1) and "text": "venue" (cell 2).
concerns.location: Section 'Appendix B - GDAC Portfolio', following the image element.

concerns.analysis: There are significant issues with meaningful visual content. First, the 'NC' agency logo present on the cover page of the source PDF is entirely missing from the HTML version. Second, the complex timeline chart in Appendix B ('GDAC Activities - Calendar Year' on page 19) is visually broken in the HTML rendering. Instead of a Gantt-style chart showing project durations across quarters, the HTML displays a few disconnected text boxes and empty table structures that fail to convey the timeline data
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC Department of Information Technology logo on page 1 and the 'GDAC Activities - Calendar Year' timeline chart on page 19.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML starts directly with text, omitting the logo. Appendix B displays a few text labels ('NC Dept of Revenue', etc.) inside boxes without the corresponding timeline bars and quarter indicators seen in the PDF.
concerns.location: Cover page and Appendix B - GDAC Portfolio (Page 19)
